chapter_context.summary: Azariah (Uzziah) reigns well in Judah but is smitten with leprosy; Shallum, Menahem, Pekahiah, and Pekah reign wickedly in Israel amidst rampant assassinations; Pekah's reign sees Assyrian deportations.

outline.text: King Azariah's (Uzziah) Good Reign in Judah; Smitten with Leprosy
outline.explanation: This section focuses on King Azariah's (Uzziah) Good Reign in Judah; Smitten with Leprosy (15:1-7), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Kings.

outline.text: Zechariah's Six-Month Reign in Israel; Assassinated by Shallum
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Zechariah's Six-Month Reign in Israel; Assassinated by Shallum (15:8-12), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Kings.

outline.text: Shallum's One-Month Reign in Israel; Assassinated by Menahem
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Shallum's One-Month Reign in Israel; Assassinated by Menahem (15:13-15), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Kings.

outline.text: Menahem's Evil Reign; Tribute Paid to Pul (Tiglath-pileser) of Assyria
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Menahem's Evil Reign; Tribute Paid to Pul (Tiglath-pileser) of Assyria (15:16-22), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Kings.

outline.text: Pekahiah's Evil Reign; Assassinated by Pekah
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Pekahiah's Evil Reign; Assassinated by Pekah (15:23-26), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Kings.

outline.text: Pekah's Evil Reign; Tiglath-pileser Captures Cities; Hoshea Slays Pekah
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Pekah's Evil Reign; Tiglath-pileser Captures Cities; Hoshea Slays Pekah (15:27-31), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Kings.
This section focuses on Jotham's Good Reign in Judah (15:32-38), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Kings.

key_themes.title: Prophetic Authority
key_themes.description: Elisha carries the mantle of Elijah, showing that God's word stands firm in judgment and mercy regardless of political power.

key_themes.title: Covenant Faithfulness and Exile
key_themes.description: The tragic fall of Samaria and Jerusalem shows that persistent rebellion and idolatry inevitably lead to judgment.

devotional.question: How do the events of Chapter 15 prompt you to trust in God's promises even during seasons of spiritual decline?
What warnings or instructions does this chapter hold for guarding your own heart against syncretism?

cultural_historical.title: The Assyrian and Babylonian Empires
cultural_historical.text: The rise of Mesopotamian super-empires (Assyria and Babylon) serves as the historical backdrop for the exile and division of the Hebrew kingdoms.
